tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, likely chronic kidney disease with possible tubular dysfunction. Low HbA1c and normal blood glucose argue against diabetes. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for possible hepatic or urinary tract involvement.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
